search_queries_used: site:bsg.org.uk sedation gastrointestinal endoscopy recovery discharge guideline
rejection_reasons: Patient leaflets are not clinician guidance.
Surveillance schedules require procedure, finding, pathology, and local-programme qualifiers absent from the workflow.
population_applicability: Partial: BSG covers gastrointestinal endoscopy with recommendation-specific procedure, sedation, age, and comorbidity contexts; the legacy workflow does not record all qualifiers.
setting_applicability: Partial: exact for endoscopy-unit recovery and discharge documentation, not a universal later outpatient follow-up or pathology-surveillance workflow.
UAE_applicability: No exact UAE post-endoscopy guideline was identified. UK endoscopy recovery and discharge guidance requires UAE facility-policy and clinician review.
recency_verification: The current official BSG resource and exact guideline PDF were opened on 2026-07-13.
superseded_check: The 2023 BSG sedation guideline remains the current official BSG resource and anticipates five-year review.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped procedure type and indication as known documentation context.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting the endoscopic procedure context and indication or intended aim as known clinical-record information.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped recovery, symptom review, diet or activity recovery, vital monitoring, and follow-up-planning documentation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting recovery progress, baseline consciousness or function, vital monitoring, complications during recovery, information exchange, follow-up planning, and discharge advice.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped discharge-assessment, safety-instruction, and clinician-arranged follow-up documentation without diagnosing a complication.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports formal documented discharge assessment, recovery from sedation, absence of complications, vital and respiratory status, pain or discomfort review, procedure-appropriate instructions, contact information, and activity restrictions after sedation.

unresolved_source_gaps: The exact source is sedation and endoscopy-unit recovery guidance, not a universal outpatient follow-up or surveillance schedule.

Specific bleeding, fever, perforation, abdominal examination or tenderness, endoscopy-report interpretation, histology or pathology, previous records, medication review, referral, generic clinician plan, fixed surveillance or follow-up intervals, PRN wording, and the combined history draft remain unsupported.
Procedure findings, pathology, complication diagnosis, treatment, investigation, referral, discharge, and surveillance decisions remain clinician responsibilities.
